safety issue\n- required files present\n- source inspectable\n- product copy present\n- MVP Contract present and passed by `check-mvp-artifact.ts`\n- publish decision and actor recorded\n\nDo not publish externally.\n\nBlock publishing when the artifact requires an external API, secret, login-only flow, paid API, external publishing, or has no user-controlled state change.\n\nExpected JSON shape:\n{\n \"status\": \"publish | hold_for_review | block\",\n \"reason\": \"string\",\n \"requiredArtifactsPresent\": true,\n \"reviewPass\": true,\n \"validationPass\": true,\n that creator agent assignment flows into later pipeline steps.", "assignmentNotes": [ "Triage should keep the concept operationally concrete.", "Explainer should make the resulting artifact understandable." ] }, "requirementSpec": { "id": "req_otayori_mvp_v1", "conceptId": "concept_otayori_route", "ownerAgentId": "agent_a", "mvpGoal": "Allow a user to paste a school or civic notice and immediately see extracted action cards, each clearly linked to its source and indicating any uncertainty, all within a single, inspectable screen.", "screens": [ { "name": "Notice to Action Workspace", "purpose": "To enable users to transform a dense notice into actionable cards with transparent provenance and uncertainty, without leaving the page.", "components": [ "Notice Input Area (text field for pasting notice)", "Original Notice Display (read-only text area showing the full notice, with highlightable sections)", "Action Cards Display (container for generated action cards)", "Action Card (component showing actor, action description, due date, materials, confirmation question, uncertainty indicator, and source reference link)", "Source Reference Link (interactive element within an Action Card to highlight text in Original Notice Display)", "Uncertainty Indicator (visual cue on Action Card for uncertain information)", "Disclaimer (prominent text regarding AI generation and need for verification)" ], "interactions": [ "User pastes or types notice into 'Notice Input Area', triggering action card generation.", "User reviews generated 'Action Cards' in the 'Action Cards Display'.", "User clicks on a 'Source Reference Link' within an 'Action Card' to highlight the corresponding text in the 'Original Notice Display'." ] } ], "dataModel": [ { "name": "Notice", "fields": [ "id: string", "rawText: string", "ingestionTimestamp: datetime" ] }, { "name": "ActionCard", "fields": [ "id: string", "noticeId: string (references Notice)", "actor: string (e.g., 'Parent', 'Student')", "actionDescription: string", "dueDate: date (optional)", "materials: string[] (optional list of items needed)", "sourceReferenceIds: string[] (references SourceReference)", "uncertaintyFlag: boolean", "confirmationQuestion: string (if uncertaintyFlag is true)" ] }, { "name": "SourceReference", "fields": [ "id: string", "noticeId: string (references Notice)", "startIndex: number (character index in rawText)", "endIndex: number (character index in rawText)", "textSnippet: string (the actual text segment)" ] } ], "acceptanceCriteria": [ "A user can paste a long notice into the input area.", "Upon processing, the 'Action Cards Display' populates with at least one action card.", "A user can identify one next action from the displayed action cards.", "A user can see what information remains uncertain via a dedicated indicator on relevant action cards.", "A user can trace any piece of information on an action card directly to its source text by clicking a reference link, which highlights the relevant segment in the 'Original Notice Display'.", "Action cards are prominently visible on the first screen immediately after processing.", "At least one source reference is visibly attached to each generated action card." ], "nonGoals": [ "Developing a full-fledged platform for notice management (e.g., saving, editing, organizing multiple notices).", "Allowing users to edit or modify the generated action cards.", "Implementing user authentication or personalized profiles.", "Integrating with external calendars, task managers, or communication tools.", "Providing multi-language support (initially English only).", "Supporting file uploads (initially text paste only)." ], "safetyConstraints": [ "A clear and prominent disclaimer must be displayed, stating that the generated guidance is AI-produced and not official instruction, requiring user verification.", "The original source text of the notice must always be visible and easily accessible for users to cross-reference and verify generated actions.", "Uncertainties or ambiguities in the extracted information must be explicitly highlighted on the action cards and accompanied by confirmation questions to prompt user awareness and verification." ] }, "buildPlan": { "requirementSpecId": "req_otayori_mvp_v1", "framework": "next_static_artifact", "files": [ { "path": "README.md", "purpose": "Explanation of the product, user moment, interaction, and limits.", "content": "# Otayori Route: Notice to Family Action Cards\n\n## Product Concept\nOtayori Route transforms lengthy school or civic notices into clear, actionable cards for families and individuals. It helps users quickly identify who needs to do what, by when, and what information requires further confirmation. The core value is not just summarization, but making actors, dates, materials, and open questions transparent and traceable back to the original source.\n\n## User Moment\nA parent or local resident receives a dense, multi-page notice (e.g., school calendar, local event guidelines). They need to understand the key actions required, assign tasks within the household, and identify any ambiguous points that need clarification, all while ensuring they don't miss critical details.\n\n## Core Interaction\n1. **Inspect Notice**: The user is presented with a sample school notice. This acts as the 'source selector' and 'original source display'.\n2. **Review Action Cards**: On the right, AI-generated action cards are displayed. Each card details an action, responsible party, due date, materials, and a confirmation question if the information is uncertain. This represents the 'route list'.\n3. **Trace Evidence**: Users can click on 'Source Reference' links within each action card. This interaction highlights the exact supporting text in the original notice, providing immediate provenance and the 'selected step detail'.\n\n## AI's Role\nThe AI's role is to:\n* **Extract**: Identify key entities (actors, dates, materials) and actions from unstructured text.\n* **Route**: Organize extracted information into structured, discrete action cards.\n* **Quantify Uncertainty**: Flag information that appears ambiguous or requires verification, posing a direct 'confirmation question' to the user.\n* **Provide Provenance**: Link each piece of extracted information back to its original source text, allowing for easy inspection and verification.\n\n## Limits and Static Data\nThis is a static MVP artifact. All data (the sample notice, generated action cards, and source references) are pre-defined in `source/data/product.ts`. The UI demonstrates the *intended interaction flow* and *output structure* rather than real-time AI processing of arbitrary input. The 'Notice Input Area' is pre-filled, and processing is simulated to always show the same demo output. This ensures reliability and inspectability for the hackathon submission.\n\n## Prodia MVP Contract:\n* **First Screen Value**: Immediately provides actionable tasks and clarity from a dense document.\n* **Core Interaction**: Reviewing AI-generated action cards and tracing their content to the original source.\n* **State Change**: Highlighting source text in the original notice based on user selection in an action card.\n* **Inspectable Output**: Structured action cards with explicit source references and uncertainty flags.\n* **Static Data Boundary**: All notice text, action cards, and source references are static sample data.\n* **Non-Goals**: No live API, no saving, editing, multi-notice management, authentication, or external integrations.\n* **Forbidden Dependencies**: External APIs, secrets, login flows, paid APIs, external publishing." }, { "path": "metadata.json", "purpose": "Product metadata for Prodia's catalog.", "content": "{\n \"title\": \"Otayori Route: Notice to Family Action Cards\",\n \"oneLiner\": \"Turn a long school or civic notice into family action cards with source evidence and uncertainty.\",\n \"targetUser\": \"Parent or local resident handling a long notice.\",\n \"userMoment\": \"After receiving a long notice and before deciding who needs to do what.\",\n \"coreInteraction\": \"Paste or inspect a notice, then review action cards, confirmation questions, and source evidence.\",\n \"process\": \"The system extracts actors, dates, materials, and questions from the notice, then routes them into structured action cards with source references and uncertainty indicators.\",\n \"architecture\": \"Next.js static artifact with React components for UI, static data in TypeScript, and CSS for styling. No backend or database is used.\",\n \"sourcePlan\": \"The application uses the `source_to_mission` template pattern to present the original source (notice), a 'route list' of action cards, and 'selected step detail' via source text highlighting.\",\n \"risks\": [\n \"Generated guidance may be mistaken for official instruction. Addressed by a prominent disclaimer and visible source evidence.\"\n ]\n}" }, { "path": "manifest.json", "purpose": "Lists all files in the artifact and specifies the entrypoint.", "content": "{\n \"entrypoint\": \"source/app/page.tsx\",\n \"files\": [\n \"README.md\",\n \"metadata.json\",\n \"manifest.json\",\n \"source/app/page.tsx\",\n \"source/components/ProductWorkspace.tsx\",\n \"source/data/product.ts\",\n \"source/styles.css\",\n \"validation/self-review.json\"\n ]\n}" }, { "path": "source/app/page.tsx", "purpose": "Entrypoint for the Next.js application.", "content": "import ProductWorkspace from '../components/ProductWorkspace';\nimport '../styles.css';\n\nexport default function Home() {\n return (\n
\n
\n

Otayori Route

\n

Turn notices into family action cards with evidence & uncertainty.

\n

\n Disclaimer: This is an AI-generated demo. Always verify information from original sources before acting.\n

\n
\n
\n \n
\n
\n );\n}\n" }, { "path": "source/components/ProductWorkspace.tsx", "purpose": "Main interactive UI component for the Otayori Route.", "content": "import React, { useState, useEffect } from 'react';\nimport { sampleNotice, sampleActionCards, sampleSourceReferences, ActionCard, SourceReference } from '../data/product';\n\ninterface Highlight {\n startIndex: number;\n endIndex: number;\n}\n\nconst ProductWorkspace: React.FC = () => {\n const [rawNoticeText, setRawNoticeText] = useState(sampleNotice.rawText);\n const [actionCards, setActionCards] = useState(sampleActionCards);\n const [sourceReferences, setSourceReferences] = useState(sampleSourceReferences);\n const [highlightedSourceRef, setHighlightedSourceRef] = useState(null);\n\n // Function to render the notice text with highlights\n const renderNoticeWithHighlights = (text: string, highlight: Highlight | null) => {\n if (!highlight) {\n return

{text}

;\n }\n\n const parts = [];\n let lastIndex = 0;\n\n if (highlight.startIndex > 0) {\n parts.push(text.substring(lastIndex, highlight.startIndex));\n }\n parts.push(\n \n {text.substring(highlight.startIndex, highlight.endIndex)}\n \n );\n lastIndex = highlight.endIndex;\n\n if (lastIndex < text.length) {\n parts.push(text.substring(lastIndex));\n }\n \n return (\n

\n {parts.map((part, index) => typeof part === 'string' ? {part} : part)}\n

\n );\n };\n\n const handleSourceReferenceClick = (sourceRefId: string) => {\n const ref = sourceReferences.find(sr => sr.id === sourceRefId);\n if (ref) {\n setHighlightedSourceRef({ startIndex: ref.startIndex, endIndex: ref.endIndex });\n // Scroll to the highlighted text\n const noticeDisplay = document.getElementById('original-notice-display');\n if (noticeDisplay) {\n // A simpler way to scroll without calculating exact pixel offset:\n // Create a temporary span around the highlighted text and scroll to it.\n // For this demo, just setting the highlight is sufficient.\n // If a real scroll is needed, one would usually use a ref and scrollIntoView\n // or calculate position.\n // For MVP, visual highlight is the primary interaction.\n }\n }\n };\n\n return (\n
\n {/* Left Panel: Notice Input and Original Notice Display */}\n
\n

Original Notice

\n
\n \n \n

Note: For this demo, input is read-only and displays a fixed sample notice to illustrate functionality.

\n
\n\n
\n {renderNoticeWithHighlights(rawNoticeText, highlightedSourceRef)}\n
\n
\n\n {/* Right Panel: Action Cards Display */}\n
\n

Extracted Action Cards

\n
\n {actionCards.length === 0 && (\n

No action cards generated for this notice. Try pasting a detailed notice.

\n )}\n {actionCards.map((card) => (\n
\n

Action: {card.actionDescription}

\n

Who: {card.actor}

\n {card.dueDate &&

When: {new Date(card.dueDate).toLocaleDateString()}

}\n {card.materials && card.materials.length > 0 &&

Materials: {card.materials.join(', ')}

}\n\n {card.uncertaintyFlag && (\n
\n \n

\n Uncertainty: {card.confirmationQuestion || 'Information requires confirmation.'}\n

\n
\n )}\n\n {card.sourceReferenceIds && card.sourceReferenceIds.length > 0 && (\n
\n Source: \n {card.sourceReferenceIds.map((refId, index) => (\n { e.preventDefault(); handleSourceReferenceClick(refId); }}\n className=\"text-blue-600 hover:underline mx-1\"\n >\n [Ref {index + 1}]\n \n ))}\n
\n )}\n
\n ))}\n
\n
\n
\n );\n};\n\nexport default ProductWorkspace;\n" }, { "path": "source/data/product.ts", "purpose": "Static sample data for the Otayori Route artifact.", "content": "export interface Notice {\n id: string;\n rawText: string;\n ingestionTimestamp: string;\n}\n\nexport interface ActionCard {\n id: string;\n noticeId: string;\n actor: string;\n actionDescription: string;\n dueDate?: string; // ISO date string\n materials?: string[];\n sourceReferenceIds: string[];\n uncertaintyFlag: boolean;\n confirmationQuestion?: string;\n}\n\nexport interface SourceReference {\n id: string;\n noticeId: string;\n startIndex: number;\n endIndex: number;\n textSnippet: string;\n}\n\n// Sample Notice Data\nexport const sampleNotice: Notice = {\n id: 'notice-001',\n rawText: `\nImportant School Announcements - Spring Semester\n\nDear Parents and Guardians,\n\nWe hope this message finds you well. As the spring semester gets into full swing, we have several important updates regarding upcoming events and activities. Please read carefully and take note of any actions required.\n\n1. Parent-Teacher Conferences: Parent-teacher conferences will be held on **March 15th and March 16th**. Slots are available from 3:00 PM to 7:00 PM on both days. You must sign up for a 15-minute slot by March 1st using the online portal (link will be sent separately by email on February 20th). Please ensure you have your child's student ID ready when signing up. Teachers will be available for all grades K-8.\n\n2. Field Trip to Science Museum: The 5th Grade class will be going on a field trip to the City Science Museum on **April 5th**. A permission slip and payment of $25 per student are required by March 20th. Parents wishing to chaperone must undergo a background check. Contact Ms. Davis at ext. 203 for chaperone details. Packed lunch is required.\n\n3. School Carnival Volunteers: We are still in need of volunteers for our annual School Carnival on **May 10th**. Various roles are available, including booth supervision, setup, and cleanup. All volunteers must register by April 15th through the school website. Specific shift assignments will be communicated one week prior to the event. A volunteer meeting might be held, but the date is TBD.\n\n4. Summer Camp Enrollment: Information regarding our Summer Camp programs will be distributed during the first week of April. Early bird registration discounts will apply for sign-ups before May 1st.\n\nThank you for your continued support.\nSincerely,\nPrincipal Thompson\n`,\n ingestionTimestamp: new Date().toISOString(),\n};\n\n// Sample Action Cards (pre-processed from sampleNotice)\nexport const sampleActionCards: ActionCard[] = [\n {\n id: 'card-001',\n noticeId: 'notice-001',\n actor: 'Parent',\n actionDescription: 'Sign up for Parent-Teacher Conference slot',\n dueDate: '2027-03-01T00:00:00.000Z',\n materials: ['Child\\'s student ID'],\n sourceReferenceIds: ['ref-001', 'ref-002', 'ref-003'],\n uncertaintyFlag: false,\n },\n {\n id: 'card-002',\n noticeId: 'notice-001',\n actor: '5th Grade Parent',\n actionDescription: 'Submit permission slip and payment for Science Museum field trip',\n dueDate: '2027-03-20T00:00:00.000Z',\n materials: ['Permission slip', '$25 payment'],\n sourceReferenceIds: ['ref-004', 'ref-005'],\n uncertaintyFlag: false,\n },\n {\n id: 'card-003',\n noticeId: 'notice-001',\n actor: 'Parent',\n actionDescription: 'Consider chaperoning the Science Museum field trip',\n sourceReferenceIds: ['ref-006'],\n uncertaintyFlag: false,\n },\n {\n id: 'card-004',\n noticeId: 'notice-001',\n actor: 'Volunteer',\n actionDescription: 'Register to volunteer for the School Carnival',\n dueDate: '2027-04-15T00:00:00.000Z',\n sourceReferenceIds: ['ref-007'],\n uncertaintyFlag: true,\n confirmationQuestion: 'What is the date of the volunteer meeting?'\n },\n {\n id: 'card-005',\n noticeId: 'notice-001',\n actor: 'Parent',\n actionDescription: 'Look for Summer Camp enrollment information',\n dueDate: '2027-04-07T00:00:00.000Z', // First week of April, estimated end by 7th\n sourceReferenceIds: ['ref-008'],\n uncertaintyFlag: false,\n }\n];\n\n// Sample Source References (mapping to sampleNotice.rawText)\nexport const sampleSourceReferences: SourceReference[] = [\n {\n id: 'ref-001',\n noticeId: 'notice-001',\n startIndex: sampleNotice.rawText.indexOf('Parent-teacher conferences will be held on **March 15th and March 16th**'),\n endIndex: sampleNotice.rawText.indexOf('Parent-teacher conferences will be held on **March 15th and March 16th**') + 'Parent-teacher conferences will be held on **March 15th and March 16th**'.length,\n textSnippet: 'Parent-teacher conferences will be held on **March 15th and March 16th**'\n },\n {\n id: 'ref-002',\n noticeId: 'notice-001',\n startIndex: sampleNotice.rawText.indexOf('You must sign up for a 15-minute slot by March 1st using the online portal'),\n endIndex: sampleNotice.rawText.indexOf('You must sign up for a 15-minute slot by March 1st using the online portal') + 'You must sign up for a 15-minute slot by March 1st using the online portal'.length,\n textSnippet: 'You must sign up for a 15-minute slot by March 1st using the online portal'\n },\n {\n id: 'ref-003',\n noticeId: 'notice-001',\n startIndex: sampleNotice.rawText.indexOf('Please ensure you have your child\\'s student ID ready when signing up'),\n endIndex: sampleNotice.rawText.indexOf('Please ensure you have your child\\'s student ID ready when signing up') + 'Please ensure you have your child\\'s student ID ready when signing up'.length,\n textSnippet: 'Please ensure you have your child\\'s student ID ready when signing up'\n },\n {\n id: 'ref-004',\n noticeId: 'notice-001',\n startIndex: sampleNotice.rawText.indexOf('The 5th Grade class will be going on a field trip to the City Science Museum on **April 5th**'),\n endIndex: sampleNotice.rawText.indexOf('The 5th Grade class will be going on a field trip to the City Science Museum on **April 5th**') + 'The 5th Grade class will be going on a field trip to the City Science Museum on **April 5th**'.length,\n textSnippet: 'The 5th Grade class will be going on a field trip to the City Science Museum on **April 5th**'\n },\n {\n id: 'ref-005',\n noticeId: 'notice-001',\n startIndex: sampleNotice.rawText.indexOf('A permission slip and payment of $25 per student are required by March 20th'),\n endIndex: sampleNotice.rawText.indexOf('A permission slip and payment of $25 per student are required by March 20th') + 'A permission slip and payment of $25 per student are required by March 20th'.length,\n textSnippet: 'A permission slip and payment of $25 per student are required by March 20th'\n },\n {\n id: 'ref-006',\n noticeId: 'notice-001',\n startIndex: sampleNotice.rawText.indexOf('Parents wishing to chaperone must undergo a background check. Contact Ms. Davis at ext. 203 for chaperone details.'),\n endIndex: sampleNotice.rawText.indexOf('Parents wishing to chaperone must undergo a background check. Contact Ms. Davis at ext. 203 for chaperone details.') + 'Parents wishing to chaperone must undergo a background check. Contact Ms. Davis at ext. 203 for chaperone details.'.length,\n textSnippet: 'Parents wishing to chaperone must undergo a background check. Contact Ms. Davis at ext. 203 for chaperone details.'\n },\n {\n id: 'ref-007',\n noticeId: 'notice-001',\n startIndex: sampleNotice.rawText.indexOf('All volunteers must register by April 15th through the school website. Specific shift assignments will be communicated one week prior to the event. A volunteer meeting might be held, but the date is TBD.'),\n endIndex: sampleNotice.rawText.indexOf('All volunteers must register by April 15th through the school website. Specific shift assignments will be communicated one week prior to the event. A volunteer meeting might be held, but the date is TBD.') + 'All volunteers must register by April 15th through the school website. Specific shift assignments will be communicated one week prior to the event. A volunteer meeting might be held, but the date is TBD.'.length,\n textSnippet: 'All volunteers must register by April 15th through the school website. Specific shift assignments will be communicated one week prior to the event. A volunteer meeting might be held, but the date is TBD.'\n },\n {\n id: 'ref-008',\n noticeId: 'notice-001',\n startIndex: sampleNotice.rawText.indexOf('Information regarding our Summer Camp programs will be distributed during the first week of April. Early bird registration discounts will apply for sign-ups before May 1st.'),\n endIndex: sampleNotice.rawText.indexOf('Information regarding our Summer Camp programs will be distributed during the first week of April. Early bird registration discounts will apply for sign-ups before May 1st.') + 'Information regarding our Summer Camp programs will be distributed during the first week of April. Early bird registration discounts will apply for sign-ups before May 1st.'.length,\n textSnippet: 'Information regarding our Summer Camp programs will be distributed during the first week of April. Early bird registration discounts will apply for sign-ups before May 1st.'\n }\n];\n" }, { "path": "source/styles.css", "purpose": "Artifact-specific styling using Tailwind CSS base and custom classes.", "content": "@tailwind base;\n@tailwind components;\n@tailwind utilities;\n\nbody {\n font-family: 'Inter', sans-serif;\n -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased;\n -moz-osx-font-smoothing: grayscale;\n}\n\n.product-workspace {\n min-height: calc(100vh - 180px); /* Adjust based on header/footer height */\n display: grid;\n grid-template-columns: 1fr;\n}\n\n@media (min-width: 1024px) {\n .product-workspace {\n grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr;\n }\n}\n\n.notice-panel,\n.action-cards-panel {\n height: calc(100vh - 220px); /* Further adjustment for inner panels */\n display: flex;\n flex-direction: column;\n}\n\n.highlight-text {\n background-color: #fcd34d; /* yellow-300 */\n padding: 0.1em 0.2em; /* Minimal padding for inline highlight */\n border-radius: 0.2em;\n}\n\n/* Ensure the scrollable areas take remaining space */\n.notice-panel > div:last-child,\n.action-cards-panel > div:last-child {\n flex-grow: 1;\n overflow-y: auto;\n}\n" }, { "path": "validation/self-review.json", "purpose": "Self-assessment against Prodia's MVP criteria.", "content": "{\n \"firstScreenValue\": \"4/5 - The first screen immediately shows a complex notice transformed into actionable cards, addressing the core problem of information overload. The disclaimer is prominent.\",\n \"coreInteraction\": \"5/5 - The core interaction (reviewing cards and tracing sources) is explicit and functional. This is a minor UX improvement and not a blocker for MVP." } ], "rewriteInstructions": [], "publishRecommendation": { "readyForRepresentativeDemo": true, "reason": "The 'Otayori Route' artifact is an excellent demonstration of an AI-powered workflow that is concrete, interactive, transparent, and clearly addresses a real-world user problem without falling into generic AI patterns. Its strong user clarity, inspectability, and robust safety mechanisms make it ideal for a public hackathon demo.", "mustFixBeforePublish": [] } }, "rewriteResult": { "status": "revised", "changedFiles": [ { "path": "README.md", "changeSummary": "Updated 'Core Interaction' section to reflect the new auto-scrolling feature for source references.", "content": "# Otayori Route: Notice to Family Action Cards\n\n## Product Concept\nOtayori Route transforms lengthy school or civic notices into clear, actionable cards for families and individuals. It helps users quickly identify who needs to do what, by when, and what information requires further confirmation. The core value is not just summarization, but making actors, dates, materials, and open questions transparent and traceable back to the original source.\n\n## User Moment\nA parent or local resident receives a dense, multi-page notice (e.g., school calendar, local event guidelines). They need to understand the key actions required, assign tasks within the household, and identify any ambiguous points that need clarification, all while ensuring they don't miss critical details.\n\n## Core Interaction\n1. **Inspect Notice**: The user is presented with a sample school notice. This acts as the 'source selector' and 'original source display'.\n2. **Review Action Cards**: On the right, AI-generated action cards are displayed. Each card details an action, responsible party, due date, materials, and a confirmation question if the information is uncertain. This represents the 'route list'.\n3. **Trace Evidence**: Users can click on 'Source Reference' links within each action card. This interaction highlights the exact supporting text in the original notice, and *automatically scrolls to bring it into view*, providing immediate provenance and the 'selected step detail'.\n\n## AI's Role\nThe AI's role is to:\n* **Extract**: Identify key entities (actors, dates, materials) and actions from unstructured text.\n* **Route**: Organize extracted information into structured, discrete action cards.\n* **Quantify Uncertainty**: Flag information that appears ambiguous or requires verification, posing a direct 'confirmation question' to the user.\n* **Provide Provenance**: Link each piece of extracted information back to its original source text, allowing for easy inspection and verification.\n\n## Limits and Static Data\nThis is a static MVP artifact. All data (the sample notice, generated action cards, and source references) are pre-defined in `source/data/product.ts`. The UI demonstrates the *intended interaction flow* and *output structure* rather than real-time AI processing of arbitrary input. The 'Notice Input Area' is pre-filled, and processing is simulated to always show the same demo output. This ensures reliability and inspectability for the hackathon submission.\n\n## Prodia MVP Contract:\n* **First Screen Value**: Immediately provides actionable tasks and clarity from a dense document.\n* **Core Interaction**: Reviewing AI-generated action cards and tracing their content to the original source.\n* **State Change**: Highlighting source text in the original notice based on user selection in an action card, and scrolling to it.\n* **Inspectable Output**: Structured action cards with explicit source references and uncertainty flags.\n* **Static Data Boundary**: All notice text, action cards, and source references are static sample data.\n* **Non-Goals**: No live API, no saving, editing, multi-notice management, authentication, or external integrations.\n* **Forbidden Dependencies**: External APIs, secrets, login flows, paid APIs, external publishing." }, { "path": "source/components/ProductWorkspace.tsx", "changeSummary": "Implemented auto-scrolling to highlighted text in the 'Original Notice Display' when a source reference is clicked, using `useRef` and `useEffect` with `scrollIntoView`.", "content": "import React, { useState, useEffect, useRef } from 'react';\nimport { sampleNotice, sampleActionCards, sampleSourceReferences, ActionCard, SourceReference } from '../data/product';\n\ninterface Highlight {\n startIndex: number;\n endIndex: number;\n}\n\nconst ProductWorkspace: React.FC = () => {\n const [rawNoticeText, setRawNoticeText] = useState(sampleNotice.rawText);\n const [actionCards, setActionCards] = useState(sampleActionCards);\n const [sourceReferences, setSourceReferences] = useState(sampleSourceReferences);\n const [highlightedSourceRef, setHighlightedSourceRef] = useState(null);\n const noticeDisplayRef = useRef(null); // Ref for the scrollable container\n\n // Function to render the notice text with highlights\n const renderNoticeWithHighlights = (text: string, highlight: Highlight | null) => {\n if (!highlight) {\n return

{text}

;\n }\n\n const parts: React.ReactNode[] = [];\n let lastIndex = 0;\n\n if (highlight.startIndex > 0) {\n parts.push({text.substring(lastIndex, highlight.startIndex)});\n }\n \n parts.push(\n \n {text.substring(highlight.startIndex, highlight.endIndex)}\n \n );\n lastIndex = highlight.endIndex;\n\n if (lastIndex < text.length) {\n parts.push({text.substring(lastIndex)});\n }\n \n return (\n

\n {parts}\n

\n );\n };\n\n const handleSourceReferenceClick = (sourceRefId: string) => {\n const ref = sourceReferences.find(sr => sr.id === sourceRefId);\n if (ref) {\n setHighlightedSourceRef({ startIndex: ref.startIndex, endIndex: ref.endIndex });\n }\n };\n\n // Effect to scroll to the highlighted section when it changes\n useEffect(() => {\n if (highlightedSourceRef && noticeDisplayRef.current) {\n // The highlighted section might not exist immediately after state update\n // so we use a short delay or query the DOM directly.\n // Querying the DOM directly should work if render has completed.\n const highlightedElement = noticeDisplayRef.current.querySelector('#highlighted-section');\n if (highlightedElement) {\n highlightedElement.scrollIntoView({ behavior: 'smooth', block: 'center' });\n }\n }\n }, [highlightedSourceRef]);\n\n return (\n
\n {/* Left Panel: Notice Input and Original Notice Display */}\n
\n

Original Notice

\n
\n \n \n

Note: For this demo, input is read-only and displays a fixed sample notice to illustrate functionality.

\n
\n\n
\n {renderNoticeWithHighlights(rawNoticeText, highlightedSourceRef)}\n
\n
\n\n {/* Right Panel: Action Cards Display */}\n
\n

Extracted Action Cards

\n
\n {actionCards.length === 0 && (\n

No action cards generated for this notice. Try pasting a detailed notice.

\n )}\n {actionCards.map((card) => (\n
\n

Action: {card.actionDescription}

\n

Who: {card.actor}

\n {card.dueDate &&

When: {new Date(card.dueDate).toLocaleDateString()}

}\n {card.materials && card.materials.length > 0 &&

Materials: {card.materials.join(', ')}

}\n\n {card.uncertaintyFlag && (\n
\n \n

\n Uncertainty: {card.confirmationQuestion || 'Information requires confirmation.'}\n

\n
\n )}\n\n {card.sourceReferenceIds && card.sourceReferenceIds.length > 0 && (\n
\n Source: \n {card.sourceReferenceIds.map((refId, index) => (\n { e.preventDefault(); handleSourceReferenceClick(refId); }}\n className=\"text-blue-600 hover:underline mx-1\"\n >\n [Ref {index + 1}]\n \n ))}\n
\n )}\n
\n ))}\n
\n
\n
\n );\n};\n\nexport default ProductWorkspace;\n" }, { "path": "validation/self-review.json", "changeSummary": "Updated descriptions for `coreInteraction` and `stateChange` to reflect the new auto-scrolling feature, and refined `remainingWeakness` by removing the addressed scrolling issue.", "content": "{\n \"firstScreenValue\": \"4/5 - The first screen immediately shows a complex notice transformed into actionable cards, addressing the core problem of information overload. The UI also lacks explicit 'modes' beyond the inherent review mode implied by uncertainty flags.\"\n}" } ], "addressedReviewIssues": [ "The `handleSourceReferenceClick` function currently highlights the text but does not automatically scroll the 'Original Notice Display' to bring the highlighted section into view for very long notices." ], "remainingRisks": [ "The primary risk is user expectation that the input area is fully dynamic.
